data.topic: Yi Jin Jing tendon changing and fascial chain remodeling for AI movement screening tool

claims.claim: SBL mapped to postures 11, 3, 10 (rear leg) via forward fold/extension loading plantar fascia through erector spinae
BFL mapped to postures 5, 12 via cross-body rotational patterns loading lat through contralateral glute
FFL mapped to postures 6, 10 via anterior chest opening loading pectoralis through contralateral adductor
Posture 11 straight knees loads full SBL; posture 8 bent knees breaks SBL at knee — exploits Anatomy Trains 'knee rule'
No peer-reviewed study has empirically validated any YJJ posture-to-chain mapping with EMG or elastography

synthesized_findings.key_insight: Yi Jin Jing postures map to validated chains by anatomical reasoning but all mappings are inference — this is the research opportunity, not the established evidence.

claims.claim: 24h sustained strain upregulates collagen synthesis; 10-min loading inhibits it
Isometric loading increases type I collagen + scleraxis; dynamic loading increases type II collagen (scar)
20s isometric holds increased tendon stiffness; 1s pulses produced zero tendon change (Kubo)
Fibroblasts go mechanically deaf after ~10 min; 6-hour refractory period (Baar)
Rest intervals between loading cycles outperform continuous loading for collagen synthesis
Sustained stretching triggers Resolvin production + fibroblast expansion up to 200% (Langevin)
15g gelatin + 50mg Vitamin C before loading doubled collagen synthesis markers (Shaw 2017)

synthesized_findings.key_insight: Loading mode determines tissue identity — isometric produces tendon, dynamic produces scar. YJJ's structure (30-60s holds, short daily bouts, rest intervals) maps onto optimal mechanotransduction protocol. But in vitro to in vivo translation is unproven.

claims.claim: 80% of acupuncture points coincide with fascial planes (Langevin 2002)
89% correspondence across 9 meridian-to-chain pairings; SBL/Bladder = 91% (Dorsher 2009)
92% overlap between 255 trigger points and acupuncture points (Dorsher 2006-2008)
VCH project 3D reconstructions show fascia network overlaps meridian trajectories

synthesized_findings.key_insight: SBL/Bladder meridian correspondence is the single best-validated pairing in the literature — independent convergence from anatomy (Wilke) and TCM mapping (Dorsher).

claims.claim: YJJ functional mobility: Hedges' g = 1.06-1.08 (10 RCTs, n=590)
YJJ + Tuina: cervical ROM +14.1°, tissue hardness -12.2% (JAMA Network Open, n=102)
YJJ improves functional ROM (g=1.06) but NOT passive flexibility (g=0.15) — tissue remodeling not muscle lengthening
Baduanjin 12 weeks improves axial muscle fascia stiffness/elasticity (direct fascial measurement)
Qigong outcomes comparable to (not superior to) structured conventional exercise in multiple comparisons

synthesized_findings.key_insight: YJJ has real clinical evidence but the loaded-ROM vs passive-flexibility distinction is the most important finding — it suggests a mechanism different from generic exercise.

claims.claim: FMS corrective exercise improves scores but does NOT reduce injury (Bahr 2016, multiple trials)
Posterior chain-specific training outperforms generic exercise for LBP (SMD ~0.6)
No RCT runs the full pipeline: screen → chain attribution → chain-specific intervention → measured outcome
Anatomy Trains framework has no independent RCT evidence as a clinical exercise protocol

synthesized_findings.key_insight: The screening-to-outcome link is broken in all existing tools. The gap is structural, not incremental — chain-level reasoning between detection and intervention is absent from every product.

claims.claim: Fascia contains ~250M sensory endings; masseter fascia 404.5 fibers/mm² vs muscle 227.6/mm²
Ruffini endings stimulated by slow deep pressure → lowers sympathetic tone (Schleip 2003)
Internal focus advantageous for proprioceptive tasks at low-moderate loads
McKay 2024: external focus advantage drops to g=0.01 (null) after correcting for publication bias
Lehman endorses Schleip's neurological model over mechanical transmission

synthesized_findings.key_insight: The neurological pathway is the strongest defense against Lehman's critique, and McKay 2024 dissolves the 'internal focus is bad' counter-argument. But if the mechanism is purely neurological, chain-specificity may not matter — the tool must measure this.

claims.claim: In vitro substrates are orders of magnitude stiffer than living tissue, pre-activating fibroblasts
~2,000 lbs force needed for 1% deformation of dense fascia lata (Chaudhry 2008)
Remote fascial displacement measured at 0.076mm — near instrument error
97-99% positive publication rate in Chinese qigong trials; only 6.8% of Chinese RCTs genuinely randomized
YJJ vs active controls (stretching, walking) shows no significant difference on primary physical outcomes
Sham qigong produces results identical to real qigong for subjective symptoms
Fascial chains may be dissection artifacts — omnidirectional 3D web selectively cut to create lines
If mechanism is global neurological response, chain-specific interventions are no more effective than generic massage

synthesized_findings.key_insight: The strongest counter-arguments target the in vitro translation gap and the inability to distinguish YJJ from generic exercise. The tool's defense is empirical measurement, not mechanistic assertion.

key_insights: SBL mapping strongest, FFL weakest
Knee rule encoded in posture design
All mappings are anatomical inference
Loading mode determines tissue identity
Baar refractory period matches YJJ practice
In vitro to in vivo gap is critical
SBL/Bladder = 91% correspondence
80% of acupoints on fascial planes
Independent convergence from Wilke and Dorsher
Functional ROM improves but passive flexibility does not
JAMA trial measures tissue hardness directly
Publication bias in Chinese trials is severe
Screening-to-outcome link is broken
Chain-specific > generic for LBP
Full pipeline has never been tested
Lehman endorses neurological model
McKay 2024 dissolves external focus argument
Yi as independent variable untested
In vitro translation gap is genuine
YJJ not superior to active controls
Publication bias is systemic not incidental
Tool defense is measurement not assertion

citations.title: Wilke et al. 2016 — What Is Evidence-Based About Myofascial Chains
Langevin & Yandow 2002 — Acupuncture points and connective tissue planes
YJJ + Tuina RCT — JAMA Network Open 2022

conflicts.topic: Whether fascial chain effects are mechanical or neurological

gemini_claim.claim: If mechanism is neurological, chain-specific interventions are meaningless — any movement produces equivalent CNS effects

claude_claim.claim: Chain-specificity can be empirically tested regardless of mechanism — if SBL postures improve SBL metrics more than generic exercise, specificity is confirmed by outcome, not mechanism

conflicts.credibility_assessment: Both positions are defensible. The tool resolves this by measuring outcomes per chain rather than asserting a mechanism.

conflicts.topic: Whether YJJ is superior to generic exercise

gemini_claim.claim: YJJ vs active controls shows no significant difference on primary physical outcomes

claude_claim.claim: YJJ improves functional ROM (g=1.06) but NOT passive flexibility (g=0.15), suggesting a different mechanism than generic exercise

conflicts.credibility_assessment: Both are from peer-reviewed sources. The loaded-ROM vs passive-flexibility distinction is the strongest evidence for a unique YJJ mechanism, but more studies needed.

gaps: No study has measured fascial chain activation during specific YJJ postures with EMG or elastography
No study has tested yi (intentional focus) as an independent variable during fascial loading
No study has run the full detect-attribute-intervene-measure pipeline
In vivo human data on fascial collagen synthesis under YJJ-specific loading is absent
Long-term follow-up (>6 months) on YJJ fascial outcomes is largely absent
Direct fascial imaging during YJJ practice has not been published
